title: The Krieg Pirates Bring War to the Baratie
description: On pages 2–5, Don Krieg’s arrival turns the Baratie crisis into a pirate siege while Nami’s departure and the looming fight split the crew’s attention.
scene: The Baratie becomes trapped between hungry enemies, terrified cooks, Krieg’s overwhelming force, and the Straw Hats’ personal priorities.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: How could our entire fleet be sunk by a single man!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: that had to be...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: the work of the Hawk-eyed man, without a doubt...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hawk-eyed Man!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: You saying that you witnessed a man,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: whose glare was as sharp as that of a Hawk doesn't necessarily prove it was him,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: But the fact that he sunk your entire fleet,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Is more than enough proof that it really was him...!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Hawk- eyed...!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Who's that?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Beats me.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: He's the man I'm searching for...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Huh!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: According to what Johnny said...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hawk-eyed man, huh...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: If it's the red-eyed man, I remember him coming to this shop before.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Right, you mean that guy who got so piss-drunk from wine that his eyes turned red.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Yeah, that idiot who caught on fire and blew up.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Heh heh, that was quite a sight.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Even that "Hawk-eyed Man" you've been looking for has been sighted there once before.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: That idiot got his information mixed up!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hm? What about Johnny?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: No, never mind.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Did he bear some ill grudge against you guys,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: for him to destroy your entire fleet like that...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I don't remember anything like that! He just attacked us all of a sudden!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Perhaps you disturbed his afternoon nap...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That's bullshit! You mean to tell me he destroyed our entire fleet for that!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: No need to get so irritated, it was just an example.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That's just the kind of place grand line is.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: A place where even the abnormal is normal, huh.

summary: This part escalates the Baratie arc from character introduction to open conflict, with Krieg threatening the restaurant and Nami pulling the story toward Arlong Park.
